metadata.sources: BBB complaint analysis, Reddit r/electricians + r/askanelectrician, NerdWallet 2026 electrical pricing, HomeAdvisor/Angi 2026, BLS SOC 47-2111, NFPA 70 (NEC 2023), NECA labor units, IRS Form 5695, DSIRE state rebates
metadata.notes: All prices reflect fully installed cost (labor + materials + permit) unless noted. Electricians are state-licensed (not federal); license reciprocity is limited. IRA 25C credit covers up to $2,000/yr for electrical work that enables a heat pump, plus a separate $600/yr panel box line when the panel upgrade is required to add a qualified heat pump, heat pump water heater, EV charger, or similar electrification upgrade.

northeast.notes: Prevailing wage, IBEW union density, historic home knob-and-tube remediation

west_coast.notes: Title 24 (CA) load calc, seismic strapping, wildfire zone breakers

midwest.notes: Higher in Chicago (Chicago EMT-conduit requirement adds 20-35%)

southeast.notes: Low labor rates; FL hurricane-zone service mast upgrades run higher

laborRates.notes: Most residential electrical work is performed by a journeyman electrician under a master's license. Master electricians personally charging $175-$250/hr are typical for custom/commercial. Emergency (after-hours) rates typically 1.5x-2x.

panel_upgrade_100_200.label: Panel Upgrade 100A to 200A
panel_upgrade_100_200.notes: Includes new 200A main breaker panel, meter base, service entrance conductors, grounding electrode system, and permit/inspection. Service mast replacement adds $400-$1,200. Utility disconnect fee may apply.

panel_upgrade_200_400.label: Panel Upgrade 200A to 400A
panel_upgrade_200_400.notes: Typically required for whole-home EV charging + heat pump + induction range + pool. Often involves a 400A meter base with two 200A panels.

service_entrance_replace.label: Service Entrance / Weather Head Replace
service_entrance_replace.notes: Replacement of service drop mast, weatherhead, and SE conductors. Utility coordination required for disconnect.

whole_house_rewire.label: Whole House Rewire
whole_house_rewire.notes: Typical range $4-$9/sqft. Required for knob-and-tube, cloth-insulated wiring, or aluminum branch circuits. Drywall repair often NOT included.

knob_tube_removal.label: Knob & Tube Removal
knob_tube_removal.notes: Many home insurers (State Farm, Allstate, Liberty Mutual, Safeco) will not renew policies with active K&T. Required for insurance and often required at sale.

aluminum_branch_remediation.label: Aluminum Branch Wiring Remediation (AlumiConn/COPALUM)
aluminum_branch_remediation.notes: CPSC-recommended: COPALUM crimp (preferred) or AlumiConn lugs at every device. Full rewire often more cost-effective on homes over 2,000 sqft.

ev_charger_level2.label: EV Charger Install (Level 2, 40-60A)
ev_charger_level2.notes: Includes 240V dedicated circuit, GFCI breaker, charger unit, and permit. Long runs (>50 ft), exterior install, or panel upgrade add cost. Many utilities offer $250-$1,500 rebates. IRA 30C credit: 30% up to $1,000 in eligible census tracts.

generator_whole_home.label: Whole-Home Standby Generator (14-26 kW)
generator_whole_home.notes: Includes generator, ATS (automatic transfer switch), concrete pad, gas line tie-in (separate plumber typical), and commissioning. Natural gas tie-in often adds $500-$2,000.

generator_portable_interlock.label: Generator Interlock Kit + Inlet
generator_portable_interlock.notes: Code-compliant alternative to a transfer switch. Uses manufacturer-specific interlock plate on panel.

gfci_outlet.label: GFCI Outlet Install
gfci_outlet.notes: NEC 210.8 requires GFCI in: bathrooms, kitchens (all countertop outlets), laundry, garages, outdoors, crawl spaces, basements, within 6 ft of any sink, dishwashers, pool/spa.

afci_breaker.label: AFCI Breaker Install
afci_breaker.notes: NEC 210.12 requires AFCI on nearly all 120V 15/20A branch circuits in dwelling units (bedrooms, living, kitchens, laundry, hallways). Breaker cost varies by brand.

heat_pump_circuit.label: Heat Pump Dedicated Circuit (30-60A)
heat_pump_circuit.notes: This circuit may qualify for 25C credit as part of the heat pump install when performed in the same tax year.

Square D (Schneider).notes: Strong residential value line; widely stocked; 20-year warranty
Industry reference line; plug-on-neutral; visual trip indicator

Eaton (Cutler-Hammer).notes: Tightest tolerances; common in pro-installed homes

Siemens.notes: Widely available at HD; solid mid-tier

GE / ABB.notes: ABB acquired GE in 2018; parts still available

SPAN.notes: Smart panel with per-circuit monitoring and load management; enables 200A service to handle 400A of loads

panels_avoid.issue: Breakers fail to trip under overload; ~2,800 fires/yr attributed
panels_avoid.action: Replace on discovery. Most insurers will not underwrite with FPE present.

panels_avoid.issue: Breakers fuse to the bus bar; bus bar corrosion
Obsolete; breakers no longer manufactured; push-button lacks trip indication
Subject to CPSC recall/alerts; overheating at bus

ev_chargers.notes: Wi-Fi, app, utility-program compatible; most utility rebates require this or Wallbox
NACS native; J1772 adapter sold separately
Strong value pick; load management built in
Enel X Way bankruptcy (2024) left some units orphaned; check cloud status

generators.notes: Market leader; widest dealer network. Past CPSC recalls on Guardian 22 kW for burn/hand-injury risk (check by serial).
Quieter; 5-year warranty standard; heavy-duty commercial-grade engines
Briggs exited standby market 2020; service via Generac/Champion channels
Diesel option for off-grid; excellent commercial reputation
Newer entrant; lower cost, growing dealer network

commonUpsells.notes: NEC 230.67 (2020+) requires SPD on dwelling service replacement/upgrade. Legitimately code-required on panel upgrades.
Convenience, not safety. Standard panel + smart breakers achieves most benefits for less.
Legitimate if panel is full; watch for labeling as 'panel rework'.

commonUpsells.item: Arc-fault breakers for entire home retrofit
commonUpsells.notes: Code only requires AFCI on new circuits and circuit extensions. Voluntary retrofit is optional.
Supplemental grounding electrode is code-required (NEC 250.53). Charging >$400 for two rods + clamp is inflated.
Cosmetic upgrade; DIY-friendly. $15-$30 DIY per switch.
Separate consideration from basic electrical; rarely needed on a service panel upgrade.

stateLicensing.notes: Electricians are licensed at the STATE level (unlike federal), with some states delegating to municipalities. License reciprocity is LIMITED. 'Journeyman' and 'Master' are the two primary tiers in most states.

AK.authority: AK Dept of Labor - Mechanical Inspection

AZ.notes: Contractor ROC license; no state journeyman card. Tucson/Phoenix have local cards.

CA.authority: CSLB C-10 Electrical Contractor + DIR ECU certification
CA.notes: No master tier. State uses General Electrician / Residential / Fire Life Safety / Voice Data Video certifications.

FL.notes: Certified Electrical Contractor (state) or Registered (local) required to contract.

GA.authority: GA Construction Industry Licensing Board (Division of Electrical Contractors)

HI.authority: HI DCCA - Board of Electricians and Plumbers

IL.authority: Local jurisdictions (no state license for most work)
IL.notes: Chicago requires Chicago electrical license; most suburbs have municipal tests.

KY.authority: KY Dept of Housing Buildings and Construction

LA.authority: LA State Licensing Board for Contractors

MD.authority: MD State Board of Master Electricians

MA.authority: MA Board of State Examiners of Electricians

MN.authority: MN Dept of Labor - Electrical Section

MO.notes: KC and STL have municipal licenses.

NJ.authority: NJ Board of Examiners of Electrical Contractors

NY.authority: Local (NYC DOB, Buffalo, etc.) - no state license
NY.notes: NYC Master Electrician license is notoriously strict.

NC.authority: NC State Board of Examiners of Electrical Contractors

RI.authority: RI Dept of Labor - Board of Electricians

TN.authority: TN Board for Licensing Contractors (CE Electrical)

TX.authority: TX Dept of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)

VT.authority: VT Division of Fire Safety - Electrical Licensing

WV.authority: WV State Fire Marshal - Electrical Licensing

WI.authority: WI Dept of Safety and Professional Services

WY.authority: WY Dept of Fire Prevention & Electrical Safety

DC.authority: DC DCRA - Board of Industrial Trades

utilityRebates.amount: up to $1,500 for panel upgrade enabling heat pump; 0% HEAT Loan
utilityRebates.eligibility: Residential; coupled with heat pump install

utilityRebates.amount: up to $2,500 panel upgrade when paired with air-source heat pump
$500 residential EV charger rebate; panel upgrade bundled with HP
$500 L2 EV charger; $1,000+ panel upgrade with heat pump
$1,500 residential L2 charger rebate (up to $5,000 multi-family)
$50 smart thermostat; limited panel incentives via IRA HEAR
$1,133 one-time credit for new L2 circuit installation
$500 L2 charger + wiring rebate; panel upgrade rebates for heat pump
up to $2,000 panel/wiring for EV and up to $5,000 TECH heat pump bundled upgrades

utilityRebates.utility: Seattle City Light / Puget Sound Energy
utilityRebates.amount: $500 L2 charger + install; panel upgrade support via HEAR

utilityRebates.utility: Energy Trust of Oregon / PGE / PacifiCorp
utilityRebates.amount: $500 L2 charger; panel upgrades via HEAR

25C.expirationNote: The 25C credit expired Dec 31 2025. Panel upgrades, heat pump enabling work, and similar 25C-eligible electrical scopes do NOT qualify for the federal credit on installs on or after Jan 1 2026. State utility rebates and IRA HEAR (income-qualified) programs may still apply.

25D.notes: Applies to solar, battery storage, geothermal, fuel cell. Electrical work integral to a qualifying solar/battery install (conduit, disconnect, panel upgrade needed to interconnect) is included in the eligible cost basis.

30C.section: 30C Alternative Fuel Vehicle Refueling Property Credit (EV charger)
30C.eligibility: Residential EV charger in eligible census tract (low-income or non-urban). Check EPA/Argonne 30C eligibility map.
30C.notes: Includes hardware AND installation labor. Claim on IRS Form 8911.

HEAR.section: DOE Home Electrification and Appliance Rebate (IRA Section 50122)
HEAR.notes: Point-of-sale rebate through state energy office. Households under 80% AMI: 100% coverage up to caps. 80-150% AMI: 50%. Rolling out state-by-state through 2026. Cannot be combined with 25C on the same expenditure.

key_sections.210.8: GFCI protection - all bathrooms, kitchens, laundry, garages, basements, outdoors, within 6 ft of sinks, dishwashers
key_sections.210.12: AFCI protection - nearly all dwelling 120V 15/20A branch circuits
key_sections.210.52: Required receptacle outlet locations and spacing
key_sections.230.67: Surge protection required on service replacement/upgrade in dwelling units (new 2020)
key_sections.230.79: Minimum service size 100A for single-family dwellings

redFlagPatterns.name: federal_pacific_zinsco_pushmatic
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Federal Pacific, Zinsco, Pushmatic, and Challenger panels have documented safety defects (breakers that fail to trip, bus-bar corrosion, recalls). Most insurers will not underwrite homes with these panels.
redFlagPatterns.action: Replace the panel. This is not optional - it is a life-safety issue. Get a 200A Square D QO, Eaton CH, or Siemens panel.

redFlagPatterns.name: no_permit_major_work
redFlagPatterns.explanation: No permit mentioned for major electrical work. Virtually every jurisdiction requires a permit for panel changes, service entrance, rewiring, EV chargers, and generators. Unpermitted work fails home inspection at resale and voids insurance.
redFlagPatterns.action: Scope must read: 'contractor pulls and pays all permits and schedules inspection.'

redFlagPatterns.name: unlicensed_handyman
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Electrical work by unlicensed individuals is illegal in virtually all states and voids homeowners insurance. State licensing protects you from shock, fire, and fraud.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require state electrician license number on the contract and verify with the state board.

redFlagPatterns.name: no_license_number
redFlagPatterns.explanation: No state electrician license number or master electrician reference on the quote. AARP's #1 home-improvement fraud category: deposits taken, work never performed.
redFlagPatterns.action: Get the state license number in writing; verify with the state licensing board before any deposit.

redFlagPatterns.name: knob_tube_not_addressed
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Knob-and-tube wiring is present but the scope does not address removal or replacement. K&T is not rated for modern loads, cannot be buried in insulation, and triggers insurance non-renewal.
redFlagPatterns.action: Scope must include full K&T removal and replacement with modern NM-B (Romex) or conduit.

redFlagPatterns.name: aluminum_branch_not_flagged
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Aluminum branch wiring (1965-1973 era) detected but no remediation mentioned. CPSC identifies this as a fire hazard. Only COPALUM crimp or AlumiConn lugs are CPSC-approved fixes.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require COPALUM or AlumiConn remediation at every device, or full rewire.

redFlagPatterns.name: no_gfci_wet_location
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Work in a location that requires GFCI protection per NEC 210.8, but no GFCI mentioned. Required in all kitchens, bathrooms, laundry, garages, outdoor outlets, basements, and within 6 ft of sinks.
redFlagPatterns.action: Scope must specify GFCI protection on all receptacles within NEC 210.8 locations.

redFlagPatterns.name: no_afci_required
redFlagPatterns.explanation: New branch circuits in dwelling units require AFCI protection per NEC 210.12, but no AFCI mentioned.
redFlagPatterns.action: Ensure AFCI breakers or dual-function AFCI/GFCI breakers are specified for all covered circuits.

redFlagPatterns.name: undersized_wire_for_amperage
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Wire gauge appears undersized for the stated breaker rating (14 AWG = 15A max, 12 AWG = 20A max, 10 AWG = 30A max, 8 AWG = 40-50A, 6 AWG = 55-65A). This is a fire hazard and a code violation.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require correct wire gauge per NEC 310.16 ampacity table. Do not accept this work.

redFlagPatterns.name: no_surge_protection_panel_upgrade
redFlagPatterns.explanation: NEC 230.67 (2020 and later) requires a Type 2 surge protective device on dwelling service replacements or upgrades. Missing from this panel upgrade quote.
redFlagPatterns.action: Confirm a Type 2 whole-house SPD is included, or that your jurisdiction has not yet adopted the 2020+ NEC.

redFlagPatterns.name: no_grounding_bonding
redFlagPatterns.explanation: No mention of grounding or bonding on a panel/service job. NEC 250 requires a full grounding electrode system (ground rods, Ufer, water line bond).
redFlagPatterns.action: Scope must include grounding electrode system verification and bonding per NEC 250.

redFlagPatterns.name: no_load_calculation
redFlagPatterns.explanation: No load calculation mentioned. NEC 220 requires a dwelling load calc for service sizing, EV chargers, heat pumps, and major additions.
redFlagPatterns.action: Ask for the written NEC 220 load calculation before signing.

redFlagPatterns.name: excessive_deposit
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Deposit above 33% on electrical work. Industry standard is 10-33% at signing with balance on substantial completion. Large deposits are a leading indicator of fraud. Several states cap home improvement deposits (MD 33%, ME 33%, CT 1/3).
redFlagPatterns.action: Cap deposit at one-third. Remainder on passed inspection.

redFlagPatterns.name: today_only_urgency
redFlagPatterns.regex: \b(today only|tonight only|expires? (today|tonight|at midnight)|sign (today|tonight|now)|valid (today|for 24 ?hours)|one[- ]time offer|limited time|manager[' ]?s? special)\b
redFlagPatterns.explanation: High-pressure urgency tactics. Legitimate electrical quotes are valid 14-60 days and breaker/wire pricing does not change hourly. Also bypasses your 3-day federal right-of-rescission mental check.
redFlagPatterns.action: Refuse to sign the same day. Get quote validity in writing (30 days minimum).

redFlagPatterns.name: vague_scope_language
redFlagPatterns.regex: \b(standard install(ation)?|per (industry|company) standard|as discussed|per conversation|tbd|to be determined|misc materials|miscellaneous)\b
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Vague catch-all scope language is unenforceable. Everything that matters must be itemized in writing.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require itemized materials and scope in writing. Verbal promises do not survive change orders.

redFlagPatterns.name: no_warranty_stated
redFlagPatterns.explanation: No workmanship warranty stated. Industry norm is 1-year workmanship minimum; reputable electricians offer 2-5 years on major work.
redFlagPatterns.action: Require written 1-year minimum workmanship warranty; 2+ years on panel, service, and generator work.

redFlagPatterns.name: trip_charge_without_credit
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Trip/service call fee not credited toward the job. Norm is for dispatch fee to apply against the job if work proceeds.
redFlagPatterns.action: Ask whether the trip charge is credited toward the work if you accept the quote.

redFlagPatterns.name: copper_to_aluminum_service_surprise
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Aluminum SE/SER is acceptable and common for service entrance and feeders (NEC 310), but must have anti-oxidant and proper lugs. Flag only if no anti-oxidant or lug treatment mentioned.
redFlagPatterns.action: Confirm anti-oxidant compound and AL-rated lugs at terminations.

redFlagPatterns.name: generator_no_gas_line_coordination
redFlagPatterns.explanation: Whole-home generator quote does not mention fuel supply. Gas line sizing and pressure drop is the #1 generator install mistake. Under-sized lines cause hard-starts and failures.
redFlagPatterns.action: Confirm a licensed plumber will size and run the fuel line. Get BTU/h demand in writing.

redFlagPatterns.name: ev_charger_continuous_load_mismatch
redFlagPatterns.explanation: NEC 625.42 treats EV charging as a continuous load: breaker must be 125% of charger output. A 48A charger requires a 60A breaker; a 40A charger requires a 50A breaker.
redFlagPatterns.action: Verify breaker size is at least 125% of the charger continuous output rating.

scopeCheckItems.label: State electrician license number on quote
Permit pulled and paid by contractor
Parts/materials itemized with brand and part numbers
Labor rate or labor total stated

recommendedScopeItems: State electrician license number on the contract
Master electrician name supervising the work
Permit pulled and paid by contractor (not homeowner)
Panel brand and model specified (Square D QO, Eaton CH, Siemens, etc.)
All breakers AFCI/GFCI where required by NEC 210.8 and 210.12
Type 2 whole-house surge protector on panel upgrade (NEC 230.67)
Grounding electrode system verified (two ground rods + water/Ufer bond)
Final inspection scheduled; payment gated on passed inspection
Drywall/plaster patching explicitly included or excluded
1-year minimum workmanship warranty (2-5 year on panel/service/generator)
Deposit capped at one-third; balance on substantial completion
questionsToAsk: What is your state electrician license number? Who is the master electrician of record?
Who pulls the permit and who is responsible for scheduling the final inspection?
Can you provide the NEC 220 load calculation in writing before we start?
What panel brand and model are you installing? (e.g., Square D QO 200A main)
Is a Type 2 surge protective device included per NEC 230.67?
Are AFCI and GFCI protections included where NEC 210.8 and 210.12 require them?
Is drywall, plaster, or finish repair included, or do I hire that separately?
What is the workmanship warranty, and is it transferable to a new owner?
Is payment contingent on passing final inspection?
What is your deposit percentage, and when is the balance due?
Is this quote valid for at least 30 days?
Will you provide documentation I need for utility rebates and the 25C tax credit?
Will your W-2 employees perform the work, or will it be subcontracted?
Does the job require a utility disconnect, and who coordinates that?
